lvgl_http_img_init.message0:
Initialize LVGL HTTP image loader screen width%1 height%2
PSRAM mode%3 Storage mode%4
Max file%5KB Timeout%6ms Debug%7
lvgl_http_img_init.tooltip: Initialize the HTTP image loader (global singleton, only needs to be called once)

lvgl_http_img_load.message0: Load image from HTTP URL%1 Display to %2
lvgl_http_img_load.tooltip: Download JPEG image from HTTP and display to LVGL Image object

lvgl_http_img_get_info.tooltip: Get the size and type information of the remote image (returns the file size, -1 means unknown)

lvgl_http_img_check.message0: Check if the image can be loaded URL%1
lvgl_http_img_check.tooltip: Check whether the image can be loaded (true: loadable, false: too large or wrong)

lvgl_http_img_has_psram.tooltip: Detect whether the system has PSRAM (larger images can be loaded when PSRAM is present)

lvgl_http_img_get_max_size.message0: Get the current maximum allowed image size
lvgl_http_img_get_max_size.tooltip: Get the maximum image size (bytes) allowed by the current configuration

lvgl_http_img_get_last_error.tooltip: Get the error code of the last operation (0=success)

lvgl_http_img_err_to_string.message0: The error code %1 is converted into a description string
lvgl_http_img_err_to_string.tooltip: Convert error codes into readable error descriptions

lvgl_http_img_cleanup.message0: Clean HTTP image loader temporary files and cache
lvgl_http_img_cleanup.tooltip: Clean temporary files and PSRAM cache to free up memory
